next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 2

Lena Morozova liked the pool at five-thirty in the morning, before the overhead lights came all the way up. The facility ran them on a schedule — dim at five, half-brightness by five-twenty, full fluorescent by six — and in that first stretch the water looked darker than it was, the lane lines softer, the ceiling lost above in shadow. She'd been swimming in pools like this since she was four years old. This one in Kirchberg was newer, cleaner, the temperature regulation tighter than the one in Zug where she'd grown up, but the fundamentals were the same: fifty meters of chlorinated water, the smell of it in the back of her throat before she even got her feet wet, lane ropes, a pace clock, the sound of her own breathing magnified under the surface into something that filled the world.

She changed in the women's room. The facility had individual changing cubicles with sensors that logged her weight, hydration level, and baseline heart rate as she stepped onto the scale, data that flowed directly to the coaching system. She didn't think about this. It had been part of her mornings for eighteen years.

The pool deck was cool under her bare feet. She walked to lane four — her lane, per the rotation schedule — and set her water bottle and pull buoy on the edge. Dara Lund was already at the wall of lane five, adjusting her goggles with small, precise movements. Three adjustments, always three: the left strap, the nose bridge, the right strap. Then she pulled her cap down over her ears and rolled her right shoulder twice, working something loose that had been recalibr...
open_hooks_to_carry: Kai wins a qualifying 200m sprint in a personal best and feels no connection to the effort
Calibration session with Yusuf reveals proprioception drift—the system initiating micro-movements ahead of Kai's conscious intent
Kai registers the gap between the time on the clock and his experience of running

setups_to_plant.description: Kai's proprioception scores show the system initiating micro-movements without conscious input—his hand reaching for things a fraction of a second before he decides to reach

state_deltas_required: Kai's disconnection from his own performance—the system does things his body should do, and the boundary is eroding
From public dominance to private uncertainty about bodily agency
